from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.templating import Jinja2Templates

# Initialize the FastAPI application
app = FastAPI(
    title="Cyberpunk Login",
    description="A basic FastAPI application for a cyberpunk-themed login page.",
    version="0.1.0"
)

# Configure Jinja2 templates
# Templates will be looked for in the 'templates' directory relative to where main.py is run.
templates = Jinja2Templates(directory="templates")

# You can add a simple root endpoint here to confirm setup, though it's not strictly required by this task description.
# It will be extended in a later task (T5_implement_login_logic).
@app.get("/", include_in_schema=False)
async def read_root():
    return {"message": "FastAPI application initialized. Templates configured."}

metadata.explanation: This code initializes a FastAPI application instance. It also configures Jinja2Templates to use the 'templates' directory, which is essential for serving HTML pages. A placeholder root endpoint is included for immediate verification of the application setup, although the actual template rendering will be implemented in a subsequent task.
